entry.title: Re: Early Numbering With Number Range Object In RAP
entry.content:
I'm not using early numbering, I just want the number determined when saving the document, but I'm having this error, any ideas?:

Statement "UPDATE " is not allowed with this status.

Error analysis

Execution takes place in a transactional context: A BO implementation
is active for "ZMRS_R_ARTIST". Statement "UPDATE <dbtab>" is therefore forbidden. In the case of
statement MESSAGE, the message text is as follows: "???" The actual error
is probably not located in the program where the termination occurs but
at a higher call level from which the program was called in an illegal
BEHAVIOR phase.

entry.title: Re: Strategies for Downtime-Optimized DMO : doDMO, doC, DMOVE2S4 for large databases
entry.content:
Thanks for putting it out clearly. For Customers already on HANA DB on-premise making move to Rise and/or public cloud hypersaler all older DMO blogs makes it confusing at a time. Blog here clarifies very well the incorporation of DMOVE2S4 with downtime optimized options. Unfortunately, for on premise ECC on Hana customers in the absence of bandwidth and latency requirement fulfillment, they had to rely on DMO with system move!

@sumitjais  I still have one question though. Based on sap blogs, It looks like DoDMO and DOC are purpose built for target S4 HANA application stack along with DB migration to HANA, Is this statement incorrect?

entry.title: Re: SuccessFactors AI - AI-Assisted Performance Insights for Manager (Set up and hints)
entry.content:
Hi Jenny,

I have followed your instructions, launched a new form and nothing appeared. However I re-read your instructions after checking my config and my config has a collaborative step at the end of year manager step (ie insights step) that includes a matrix mgr so this is going to be a big of an issue for us as I can see from your instructions this can only be a single step. This will make the functionality not suitable as this will restrict clients the ability to have multiple reviewers at end year step. Can this please be highlighted as client will now have to relook at licencing usage for this feature as this is not clear in any of the sales documents for AI for performance.

Thanks

Mark

entry.title: Re: Problems installing SQL Anywhere 17
entry.content:
These issues will also be encountered for any SQL Anywhere window patch Build 7254 or newer.  

The MSVC Runtime requirement is documented. It is contained in the release notes (readme_EBF.html as Engineering Case 827584) as well as in the readme_xx.txt (where xx is the 2 character language code). Note that you can see the install image by loading the installer executable (which is a Package for the Web image)  in a tool like winrar.

Both document the following:

The SAP SQL Anywhere installation requires that the following Microsoft Visual
C++ Redistributable Packages be installed on the current systems before
running the installer.

X86 https://aka.ms/vs/17/release/vc_redist.x86.exe
X64 https://aka.ms/vs/17/release/vc_redist.x64.exe

These are compatible with Visual Studio 2015, 2017, 2019, and 2022.

I know the installer reports the missing runtime as an error but that should not result in a crash.  

You should not set the classpath for SQL Central (or any of the java tools in SQLA).  The executable for the java tools constructs the pathing and java start line based on the SQLANY17 environment variable.

If the install has failed, rerunning the install in Repair mode, after addressing the cause of the failure, should be sufficient to address the startup problem with SQL Central, which was the result of the missing MSVC runtime.

entry.title: Re: Problems installing SQL Anywhere 17
entry.content: @chris_keating, it should report an error before trying to install the program, not in the middle of the install. And what about the 2 JAVA issues reported?

entry.title: Re: Architecting a Secure API Bridge: Automating SAP Datasphere Data Consumption via OAuth 2.0/Postm
entry.content:
@senthil17 : 

Thank you for the feedback — glad you found the explanation helpful!

Great question — the choice between Technical User, API Access, and Interactive Usage depends on how the OAuth client will be used.

Technical User (Used in this article)
This is designed for system-to-system communication using a non-human identity.
It is the recommended option when using OAuth Client Credentials, as demonstrated in this article.
Typical use cases include Postman automation, scripts, middleware, and scheduled integrations that require no user interaction.

API Access
This is typically used in API-centric or API-managed scenarios, often in conjunction with API management tools.
Depending on the configuration, it may also work for programmatic access, but it is not the typical choice for Client Credentials–based automation.

Interactive Usage
This is intended for user-driven authentication flows, where a human logs in via a browser.
It is not suitable for Postman-based automation, since it requires user interaction.

Will all three work with Postman?
For the automated OAuth Client Credentials scenario shown in this article, Technical User is the recommended and commonly used configuration, while Interactive Usage is not suitable for automation workflows.

Hope that helps.

entry.title: S/4HANA 2025におけるALV更新（REFRESH_TABLE_DISPLAY）実行時のエラーについて
entry.content:
S/4HANA 2025環境にて、ALVのデータ更新処理を行う際にエラーが発生しております。
つきましては、以下の2点の実装方式における原因と解決策をご教示ください。
関数型ALV（REUSE_ALV_GRID_DISPLAY）
オブジェクト指向型ALV（CL_GUI_ALV_GRID）

entry.title: Re: Problems installing SQL Anywhere 17
entry.content: The JAVA errors are most likely due to the missing MSVC as far as I can recall.

entry.title: SAP B1 GRPO posting wrong amount to Inventory acct for Serial/Batch Valuation Method
entry.content:
In SAP Business One, we are using the Serial/Batch Valuation Method, which is designed to track costs at the specific batch level. SAP states that if the same batch number is received multiple times with different costs, the batch cost may appear to become an "average".

We have 3 Goods Receipt POs (GRPO) for an item with batch UMC. GRPO 2500082 with unit price $21,000, GRPO 2600109 with unit price $22,500 and GRPO 2600133 with unit price $22,500. Each GRPO has 1 qty of the item.

The 1st GRPO 2500082 has cost $21,000 recorded in the Batches and Serial Inventory Audit Report, which is correct.

The 2nd GRPO 2600109 has cost $21,750 recorded in the Batches and Serial Inventory Audit Report, which is also correct. Since average cost is ($21,000 + $22,500) / 2 qty = $21,750.

However the 3rd GRPO 2600133 has cost $22,250 recorded in the Batches and Serial Inventory Audit Report, which looks wrong. The average cost should be ($21,000 + $22,500 + $22,500) / 3 = $22,000.

So why did SAP record $22,250 as the cost, instead of $22,000?

entry.title: SAP Build Process Automation: My Inbox stuck in loading
entry.content:
Good day,

There seems to be an issue currently with the My Inbox in SAP Build/SAP Build Process Automation. We are receiving an error 403 when viewing the My Inbox page. Our destinations are properly defined, and have not made any changes since Friday. It still persists up to this day. 

Anyone also experiencing this and have applied a fix?

 

Thank you very much!

entry.title: using BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE to perform a 102 reversal
entry.content: When using BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE to perform a 102 reversal, the input parameters REF_DOC_YR, REF_DOC, and REF_DOC_IT are provided in BAPI2017_GM_ITEM_CREATE. However, in the generated material document, the fields SJAHR, SMBLN, and SMBLP are blank when checked in MSEG Question: Besides providing the values for REF_DOC_YR, REF_DOC, and REF_DOC_IT in GOODSMVT_ITEM, which additional field in GOODSMVT_ITEM must be filled so that SJAHR, SMBLN, and SMBLP can also be generated?

entry.title: 🇮🇳 SAP CodeJam ABAP: SAP BTP, ABAP Environment & RAP ( Pune, India )
entry.content:
This SAP CodeJam event is on the topic of the ABAP Cloud, and specifically introduces attendees to ABAP Cloud and the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model. ABAP Cloud is the ABAP-based development model for S/4HANA Cloud, S/4HANA on-premise, and SAP BTP, ABAP environment.

Learn how to use proven and new technologies, such as Core Data Services (CDS) and behavior definition and implementation languages, to build SAP Fiori apps and OData services on any of the ABAP Cloud options. In this hands-on experience, developers will create their first ABAP applications using ABAP Cloud, expose and consume services via HTTP, and explore the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model.

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Tuesday 26th May 2026 in Pune from 09:00 - 15:30 IST. The language and content of the event will be in English.

Location: Bloomberg Data Services (India) Private Ltd, SkyOne, S. No. 210, 5th Floor, Kalyani Nagar, Village Yerwada, Pune, IN, 411006.

The event is kindly hosted by Bloomberg Data Services (India) Private Ltd. Thank you! Please note that spaces are limited - we can only accommodate 25 attendees. If you wish to attend, register by clicking the Yes button in the Will you be attending? section above to the right. A few weeks before the event, we will ask you to confirm your attendance.

The instructor is Developer Advocate @Shilpa_Shankar

There are essential prerequisites that must be completed by every attendee, before arriving at the event location. They are documented here: abap-exercises-codejam/prerequisites.md at main · SAP-samples/abap-exercises-codejam (github.com).

For more information on SAP CodeJam events, to ask questions, and to chat with your fellow attendees, head over to the SAP CodeJam group.

entry.title: MF60 can not generate replenishment with 313 movement type ?
entry.content:
Dear sap,

We want to use 2 steps of  production GI from WM storage location as below:

The warehouse(WM managed storage location) dispatches goods to the production line  stock(non-WM managed storage location) based on the production picking list via MF60.

Our operational steps are as follows:

1. Set the configuration 'Define Control Data for Pull List' with movement 313.

2. Goods replenishment via MF60 with 2 materials, one is from storage location with WM managed, the other is from storage location with non WM managed.

3. When we click 'STAGE', material with WM managed is  successful, it creates the outbound delivery , but the outbound delivery's movement is 411 ?

And the other material with non WM managed, it occurs error： E M7 096 Goods movement not possible with mvmt type 313.

So my question is as below:

1. Why the second material can not be staged with 313 movements?

2. How could i use 2 steps of  production GI from WM storage location?

 

Thanks.

Ying

entry.title: Project Setup Agent | SAP Business AI | Agentic AI | EPPM
entry.content:
Agent Overview

The AI-driven solution allows front office project managers to quickly create projects based on similar past projects without navigating the complex user interfaces or having to contact back office PMO support. It also facilitates the quick allocation of key resources required to launch projects effectively. This solution enables project manager to spend less time on operational coordination and dedicate more time on improving project profitability and driving operational efficiency.

 
Value proposition
  • Leverage similar past​ projects to set up new projects
  • Staff key resources quickly

Benefits

  • 10% reduction in time spent to create project
  • 16% reduction in ​time spent on resource allocation
  • 30% reduction in time spent reworking projects created from incorrect templates

Applicable for customers on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition with Joule.

More information  : Project Setup Agent

entry.title: Re: VAT Changes as of January 1st, 2024 in Switzerland
entry.content:
Hi

Thank you for the information above. We wanted to take your help on the following:

1. Seemingly the Customers with a UID will need to be maintained with the number (without the mask) in the BP master data with the tax category as CH1. CH2 would hold the VAT number of the customer. Is that correct ?

2. When we need to print the UID number on forms and send it in EDI transactions, we need to get the correct format of the UID. This would be a custom development that every client needs to do and there is no standard method / function provided in order to extract the information from the SAP system.

Please advise.

Appreciate your help

entry.title: [S4HANA Cloud] How to automatically suggest default storage locations for sub-items in each MRP area
entry.content:
Please explain BOM deployment in MRP areas.
My understanding of BOM deployment priorities is as follows:
■ Priority
1. BOM item storage location
2. Copy from the production storage location of the child item's material master (MARC)
3. Parent item's MRP group setting
→ Issue storage location category 1: Copy from the production storage location of the child item's material master (MARC)
*Ideally, it should be copied from the production storage location of the MRP area profile (MDMA)
→ Issue storage location category 2: Copy from the production storage location of the parent item's material master

The requirement is that the default issue storage location of child items should be flexibly changed for each parent item's MRP area, and planned orders should be created accordingly.
Currently, the following solutions come to mind, but I would like to avoid them due to the difficulty of master maintenance. Is it possible to achieve this through CBC settings, etc.?
■Current Solution
- Prepare multiple versions of the BOM
- Manage the material master with a different code for each production storage location

Example)
If both the parent and child items have the following MRP area and storage location settings:
- MRP Area 151A → Storage Location 151A
- MRP Area 151B → Storage Location 151B

If the parent item's requirement arises in MRP Area 151A, the storage location for the planned order's components should be 151A. If the requirement arises in MRP Area 151B, the storage location for the planned order's components should be 151B.

entry.title: Logistics Invoice Verification in SAP MM: End‑to‑End Guide with Practical Insights
entry.content:
Author’s Note
My name is Sandireddy Lakshmi Triveni, and I am a final-year student in the Computer Science and Engineering
(CSE) department at Chalapathi Institute of Engineering and Technology, located in Guntur, Andhra Pradesh,
India. I am pursuing a Bachelor of Technology (B.Tech) degree, which is an undergraduate engineering program
in India, comparable to a Bachelor of Engineering in many other countries.


Throughout my academic journey, I have developed a strong interest in enterprise systems and ERP technologies,
particularly SAP. As part of my coursework, I have worked on practical exercises and simulations using SAP
Materials Management (MM), a module that manages procurement and inventory processes. These activities
were conducted in a training environment on SAP S/4HANA, primarily through the SAP GUI interface. Although
S/4HANA supports modern Fiori applications, our training emphasized SAP GUI since it continues to be widely
used in many organizations.


I also completed a project covering the end-to-end procure-to-pay cycle, which included configuration and
testing of Logistics Invoice Verification (LIV), tolerance limits, and message controls. This hands-on experience
allowed me to understand how Material Management integrates with Financial Accounting (FI). For example,
when a purchase order was processed in MM, the system automatically updated the corresponding financial
postings in FI. This automation made the workflow efficient and highlighted the importance of accurate data
entry.


Working with transactions in INR (₹, Indian Rupees) also gave me exposure to how SAP systems handle currency
localization. To ensure confidentiality, I blurred sensitive details such as company codes in screenshots used for
documentation.


I am a SAP Certified Associate in End-to-End Business Processes for the Intelligent Enterprise, and I am
currently preparing for the SAP Sourcing and Procurement certification to strengthen my expertise further.
Reflecting on these experiences, I realized that even minor errors in procurement entries could ripple into
financial records. This taught me the importance of precision, data integrity, and cross-functional awareness
lessons that I believe will be invaluable as I transition from academic learning to professional practice.


I first came across Logistics Invoice Verification (LIV) while working on SAP MM exercises and project
simulations. During these sessions, I realized how crucial LIV is in preventing mismatches between purchase
orders, goods receipts, and vendor invoices. This motivated me to document my learnings and share them with
peers and the wider SAP Community.


You can also read my Hello SAP Community introduction post here : Click Here

 

Introduction
Logistics Invoice Verification (LIV) is the final step in the procure to pay cycle within SAP Materials
Management (MM). It ensures vendor invoices are validated against purchase orders and goods receipts before
payment is released. This process safeguards organizations against duplicate payments, incorrect amounts, and
compliance issues, while seamlessly integrating with Financial Accounting (FI).


Business Context


In procurement, mismatches between purchase orders, goods receipts, and vendor invoices are common. Without
proper verification, such discrepancies can lead to financial losses and strained vendor relationships.

SAP’s Logistics Invoice Verification addresses these challenges by:


-->Validating invoices against purchase orders and goods receipts.
-->Automatically detecting variances in price or quantity.
-->Blocking incorrect invoices until reviewed and released.
-->Generating accounting entries in FI for accurate financial reporting.


Key Features of LIV
• Invoice Posting Options: PO based, GR based, and service entry sheet verification
• Automatic Checks: Price, quantity, and currency validations against PO/GR data.
• Tolerance Limits: Configurable thresholds allow minor differences without blocking invoices.
• Blocked Invoices: Invoices exceeding tolerance limits are blocked until reviewed.
• Integration with FI: Verified invoices generate accounting documents for vendor liability and payment
scheduling.


Configuration Steps
1. Tolerance Limits (T-code: OMR6)
Tolerance limits control acceptable variances during invoice verification. They ensure that minor differences
between purchase orders, goods receipts, and vendor invoices do not block postings unnecessarily, while larger
discrepancies trigger warnings or errors.


These are the tolerance limits that have configured
• AN/AP: Controls invoice amounts with/without PO reference. Prevents excessive postings.
• BD: Allows small rounding differences to post automatically, avoiding unnecessary blocks.
• DQ: Blocks invoices with quantities exceeding GR, ensuring procurement accuracy.
• PP: Ensures vendor price changes don’t slip through unnoticed.

Figure 1 Tolerance Limits
NOTE: Sensitive identifiers blurred for confidentiality; values shown are from a training environment.

And the main tolerance limits used in Logistics invoice verification are
1. AN/AP – Amount limits with or without PO reference

Figure 2 AN/AP – Amount limits with or without PO reference
NOTE: Sensitive identifiers blurred for confidentiality; values shown are from a training environment.

Figure 3 AN/AP – Amount limits with or without PO reference
NOTE: Sensitive identifiers blurred for confidentiality; values shown are from a training environment.

2. BD – Small differences automatically posted


Figure 4 BD – Small differences automatically posted
NOTE: Sensitive identifiers blurred for confidentiality; values shown are from a training environment.

3. DQ – Exceeded Quantity Variance

Figure 5 Exceeded Quantity Variance
NOTE: Sensitive identifiers blurred for confidentiality; values shown are from a training environment.

4. PP – Price variance

Figure 6 Price variance
NOTE: Sensitive identifiers blurred for confidentiality; values shown are from a training environment.

2. Message Controls (T-code: OMRM)
Message controls are configured in transaction OMRM, and organizations typically set stricter controls for
financial risks (e.g., price variances as errors) while allowing operational flexibility for minor issues (e.g.,
currency differences as warnings).


Error (E): Blocks invoice posting until corrected. Used for critical mismatches like price variance.
Warning (W): Allows posting but alerts the user. Common for currency differences.
Information (I): Provides a message without blocking. Useful for audit trails or user awareness.

Figure 7 Message Controls (T-code: OMRM)


The effect of message controls can be seen in the case studies for instance, a currency mismatch triggers a
warning, while a price variance triggers an error


CASE STUDY

1. Price Variance (PP Key)
A Purchase Order is created at ₹100 per unit, and Goods Receipt is posted. Vendor
submits an invoice in MIRO at ₹120 per unit.
Since the price variance exceeds the PP tolerance limit, the system blocks posting with: “Price too high
(tolerance limit exceeded)”.

Figure 9 Purchase order
NOTE: Sensitive identifiers blurred for confidentiality; values shown are from a training environment.

Figure 8 Invoice
NOTE: Sensitive identifiers blurred for confidentiality; values shown are from a training environment.


2. Quantity Variance (DQ Key)
Create a Purchase Order for 100 units and post a Goods Receipt for 100 units.
In MIRO, enter an invoice for 120 units.
The system compares invoice vs GR and blocks posting with: “Quantity invoiced greater than goods receipt
quantity”.

Figure 10 Error


3. Currency Difference (Message 095)
Create a Purchase Order in INR.
In MIRO, enter the invoice in USD.
The system issues a non‑blocking warning: “Currency entered differs from PO currency”, and posting is still
possible.

Figure 11 Warning


Logistics Invoice Verification (LIV) is a critical safeguard in the procure-to-pay cycle, ensuring accuracy,
compliance, and financial control. By configuring tolerance limits and message controls, organizations can
balance operational flexibility with risk prevention.
In this article, I covered:
• The business context of LIV
• Key features such as tolerance limits and message controls
• Case studies on price variance, quantity variance, and currency differences


As a student working through SAP MM projects and simulations, documenting these scenarios has helped me
deepen my understanding of how SAP integrates procurement with financial accounting. This learning journey
also complements my certification path as a SAP Certified Associate in End-to-End Business Processes,
currently preparing for the SAP Sourcing and Procurement certification, I aim to apply these insights to
real-world consulting challenges.


I welcome feedback, suggestions, and discussions from the SAP Community to refine my knowledge further and
contribute more practical insights. Please share your thoughts in the comments or connect with me to exchange
ideas!

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud enables user-based authorization control for sales price by products
entry.content:
Dear expert,

Please advise how to implement the following requirements in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.

SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud enables user-based authorization control, allowing sales staff to access only sales prices, pricing condition, sales orders and analytics reports of products they are responsible for.

In our scenario, two sales users belong to the same sales organization and are assigned the same business role.

Sales staff A is responsible for Material 1, while Sales staff B is responsible for Material 2.

Sales A shall only be authorized to edit/view the sales price of Material 1 and shall not have access to the price of Material 2.

Best regards,

Joey

entry.title: [S4HANA Cloud] How to automatically suggest default storage locations for sub-items in each MRP area
entry.content:
Please explain BOM deployment in MRP areas.
My understanding of BOM deployment priorities is as follows:
■ Priority
1. BOM item storage location
2. Copy from the production storage location of the child item's material master (MARC)
3. Parent item's MRP group setting
→ Issue storage location category 1: Copy from the production storage location of the child item's material master (MARC)
*Ideally, it should be copied from the production storage location of the MRP area profile (MDMA)
→ Issue storage location category 2: Copy from the production storage location of the parent item's material master

entry.title: Re: YCOA Account Groups - Verification of Usage and Difference between ABST and RECN
entry.content: Dear Henrike Thank you for help.

entry.title: Re: Strategies for Downtime-Optimized DMO : doDMO, doC, DMOVE2S4 for large databases
entry.content: @SAP4VMG doC targets to S/4HANA, while doDMO does not have such limitation and can be incorporated for ECC as well. Unlike DMO with system move & like DMO, both doC and doDMO utilize memory-pipes for data transfer, and hence migrate DB only, upon which new DB stays connected to existing SAP application only. However, new set of SAP applications can be setup and connected to migrated DB explicitly.

entry.title: [S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition] Custom Currency Code for Separate Exchange Rate Management - Feasibil
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I have a question regarding currency and exchange rate configuration in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

**Current Situation (ECC)**
- We have two company codes: one in the US and one in Hong Kong
- Both use USD as the document currency
- However, they need to apply different exchange rates for USD→JPY conversion
- In our current ECC system, we created a custom currency code (e.g., "USDA") to manage separate exchange rates for the Hong Kong entity

**Questions for S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition**
I would like to understand the feasibility and best practices for this requirement:

1. **Can we create custom currency codes (like "USDA") in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?**
- Is this supported via standard configuration or key user extensibility?

2. **If custom currency codes are supported, can we maintain separate exchange rate tables for them?**
- Can we configure exchange rates for the custom currency code independently from the standard USD?

3. **Are there any functional limitations or restrictions when using custom currency codes?**
- Are there any standard apps, processes, or integration scenarios that do not support custom currency codes?
- Any known issues with reporting, intercompany transactions, or payment processing?

4. **Is there an alternative standard approach recommended by SAP for this scenario?**
- For example, using different currency types or other configuration options?

Any guidance or documentation references would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance!

entry.title: Re: Create purchase requisition for Unplanned components...
entry.content: I have already indicated that the system will not automatically create purchase requisitions for newly added BOM components once consumption has already been posted. The only way to generate PRs for those shortages is to re-run MRP (either single-item or plant-wide). If this happens often, you can automate it by scheduling frequent background MRP runs or implementing a BAdI/Enhancement to trigger a single-item MRP run whenever new components are added to a released production order.

entry.title: Re: [S4HANA Cloud] How to automatically suggest default s...
entry.content:
The system does not automatically copy issue storage locations from the parent’s MRP area profile (MDMA). This means that planned orders will not flexibly adjust component issue locations per parent MRP area without extra configuration.

One option is to maintain BOMs with different issue storage locations per MRP area but this will involve high maintenance effort.

If flexibility is critical, avoid multiple BOMs or duplicate materials. Instead, implement a custom enhancement that dynamically assigns issue storage locations based on the parent’s MRP area during BOM explosion. This ensures planned orders reflect the correct storage location without excessive master data maintenance.

entry.title: How to prevent Unplanned Cross Plant Goods issue and Goods receipt w.r.t. order in MIGO？
entry.content:
Hi Gurus,

We need to prevent Unplanned Cross Plant Goods issue and Goods receipt in MIGO linked with Order (261/262)

The approach is to compare the Material Plant (MIGO‘s Where tab) with Order's Plant. In case of difference, error is thrown. 

1. Failed auth. object. M_MSEG_LGO- it is for Material's plant and Storage location

2. Failed BADI_MMIM_CHECK_MATDOC_ITEM.  

There are 3 conditions as constants: Plant, Movement type and Order category. The logic seems not OK.

Please kindly share your experience and thought.

Many thanks,

LL

entry.title: Re: Onboarding without PDC step
entry.content: Hello thank you for your reply, I fully agree but as this was asked by Business just wanted to check others comments 🙂

entry.title: Re: API of Pack/ Unpack Handling Unit in SAP Public Cloud...
entry.content: In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, to my knowledge, there is no direct “Pack/Unpack Handling Unit” API tied to the HU02 app. Packing and unpacking are only possible through the Handling Unit service API (API_HANDLING_UNIT_SRV), which lets you create, update, or delete Handling Units and assign materials to them. The limitation is that the system does not expose HU02 actions as separate APIs. So you must replicate them by updating or removing HU items via the Handling Unit API, always referencing the inbound delivery through API_INBOUND_DELIVERY_SRV

entry.title: best-priced items in Compare Supplier Quotation app
entry.content:
Dear Community,

I have some question about the determination logic in best-priced items app Compare Supplier Quotation.

In SAP Help document related to this app (Compare Supplier Quotations | SAP Help Portal) Items with the lowest price are displayed as best-priced items. Any other factors such as supplier evaluation score, are not included in the calculation.

I want to ask this logic to community members.

1) I created RFQs with three item
R001 10EA, R002 10EA, R003 10EA with same delivery date.

2) I created Supplier Quotation with Supplier A with
R001, 9EA, 950/EA for price
R002, 10EA, 1000/EA for price
R003, 10EA, 1050/EA for price

3) Also, created Supplier Quotation with Supplier B with

R001, 10EA, 970/EA for price

R002, 10EA, 1000/EA for price

R003, 10EA, 1030/EA for price.

4) Only concerns the price for item level, the best-priced items will be 2(950 and 1000) for Supplier A and 2(1000, 1030) for Supplier B.
But in my system, best-priced items for supplier quotation A is 1 and best-priced items for supplier quotation B is 3

5) Determination logic for best-priced items concern it is fully delivered or not?

Kindly help any advice if have knowledge about this issue.
Best Regards,

entry.title: Re: Transfer standard field from erp delivery to ewm deli...
entry.content: The BAdI is correct, but you will need an ERP-side enhancement to pass ABLAD at item level, since the standard delivery item (LIPS) does not contain it. This way, EWM can consume it per item consistently.

entry.title: My Purchase Requisition becomes Advance license
entry.content:
Dear Sir / Madam, We are using My purchase requisition App in business catalogue 

SAP_MM_BC_SSPPR_MANAGE_PC  which was self service , but now we notice that become in Advance category.  So in which app we create purchase requisition with Self service category.

 

Thanks and regards 

Senthil

entry.title: Re: Error "/SCWM/L3690: Enter a destination HU" during RF...
entry.content: Follow the "Resolution" listed in SAP note 3683950

Hello,

I am currently facing an issue with My Inbox page of SAP Build Process Automation. It is currently not loading the tasks that are triggered in BPA and is just stuck in loading. Is there any fix for this?

Thank you very much!

entry.title: Is PO Creation via RAP (EML) using I_PURCHASEORDERTP_2 Possible in S/4HANA Public Cloud?
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

In SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, is it possible to create Purchase Orders through a RAP application using EML by consuming the standard business object interface I_PURCHASEORDERTP_2, based on data received from an external system (e.g., via Excel upload)?

Please let me know if this approach is supported or not.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: 2605 C4C Release Content
entry.content:
Hi SAP,

when will 2605 C4C Release Content be published?

Cheers,

Pavla

entry.title: Re: How to prevent Unplanned Cross Plant Goods issue and...
entry.content: For preventing unplanned cross‑plant goods issues/receipts in MIGO (261/262), the correct place to implement the check is BADI_MMIM_CHECK_MATDOC_ITEM. The key is to refine the condition handling at item level. Compare the plant in MATDOC with the order’s plant (AUFK/AFKO), and only trigger the error when both the movement type (261/262) and the order category (production order) match.

entry.title: Regarding Retention Period Limit in SAP Cloud ALM Export Reports
entry.content:
Dear Team,

I am trying to increase the retention period in CALM for export reports; however, the system is not allowing us to set the limit above 14 days.

Could you please confirm if there is any way to increase this retention limit in SAP Cloud ALM? If there are any configurations or alternative approaches available, kindly guide us accordingly.

 

Best Regard,

Shubham Singh Rao

entry.title: BADI for Billing document Item Custom Field

1) we have added few custom fields at item level, now we want to fill data via BADI while saving billing Document, but we are not able to find any relevant BADI for the same.

2) we want to validate few fields at the time of saving billing Doc, again not able to find BADI for the same

entry.title: Re: AP Aging Query Not Matching SAP Business One Aging Re...
entry.content:
Hi,

Try joining the OITR and ITR1 table keeping the ReconDate as the consideration to get the correct date on which the document was closed.

Kr,

Jitin

entry.title: Consultation on SAP System Integration Methods (e.g., OData, RFC, IDOC, etc.)

While for our side we can't confirm it is correct or not, so we want to confimr with SAP.

 

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Do we need an SAC Agent if my BW source system is hos...
entry.content:
Hi coolmanjus,

 

Yes, you need to install both SAP Cloud Connector and SAC agent on same SAP Cloud Environment, where the agent could reach to the BW system in same network.

 

Best regards,
Xiwen

entry.title: Re: Mov 712 in EWM - counterpart from ERP
entry.content:
MON shows warehouse tasks, warehouse orders, and stock movements relevant to EWM. A pure ERP transfer posting may not generate an EWM document number unless it triggers a warehouse process.
Ensure the ERP posting was actually sent to EWM. If the queue failed, the document won’t appear in MON.
In customizing, confirm that 712 is mapped to an EWM process type. If it is not, EWM won’t generate a corresponding document.
So in short, if you don’t see the 712 movement in MON, it is likely because the movement type is not integrated to EWM by default. You will need to check the customizing for ERP–EWM movement type mapping and ensure the queue transfer is working.

entry.title: Re: Observer Design Pattern – Real-Time Use Cases
entry.content: You can simplify pattern's syntax by creating a custom BADI. Each observer will be a BADI implementation, and your subject will use simple commands like GET BADI / CALL BADI to notify observers.

entry.title: Re: Required Fiori Usage Statistics
entry.content:
Hello,

I understand your need for a solution to track usage statistics for Fiori applications within the NW AS ABAP system. At this time, there is no dedicated tool delivered by SAP for this specific task. However, there are several resources available that outline best practices and alternative approaches to retrieve this information:

- 3203036 - Tracking usage of Fiori apps https://me.sap.com/notes/0003203036
- 2629143 - Best practices to gather Fiori usage statistics for on-premise systems https://me.sap.com/notes/0002629143

Fiori Tracker: Tiles usage and User monitoring for SAP Fiori launchpad (Monitoring SAP Fiori Launchpad)

Apps usage report for SAP Fiori launchpad (Monitoring SAP Fiori applications)

Some of these options involve custom or third-party solutions, which are not part of the standard offerings from SAP.
Additionally, there is a helpful SAP-delivered application within the SAP Cloud ALM product that may address your needs.
You can find more details about this solution at the following link:
Real User Monitoring.

Best Regards,
Joe

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud enables user-based authoriza...
entry.content:
This is achieved through a combination of:

1) Business Role Templates
Assign roles such as Sales Representative or Sales Pricing Specialist, which restrict access to specific apps and data objects.

2) Authorization Objects
Control access at the level of sales prices, pricing conditions, sales orders, and analytics reports. For example, you can restrict visibility to only the products or sales organizations the user is responsible for.

3) Analytical Privileges
Limit reporting data so users only see KPIs and analytics for their assigned product groups or sales areas.

4) Flexible Restrictions
Use organizational assignments (sales org, distribution channel, division, product responsibility) to dynamically filter what each user can access.

entry.title: Re: Time Valuation for 4 Cycle Shifts | SAP SuccessFactor...
entry.content: Hi Soraia. Yes, I can use Time Containers to calculate the hours. However, I wanted to check if there’s a way to deduct hours from the container as well. For example, if 18 hours are accumulated in week 1 and then used in week 2 (under Basic and OT), I would like the balance to reset to 0 by week 3. At the same time, I’m considering setting up the Time Container for a 4-week period, as there may be scenarios where surplus or deficit hours need to be adjusted in the 3rd or 4th week too. Thanks, Saif

entry.title: Re: How to create a Tunnel connection to Datasphere in SAC?
entry.content:
Hi Julia,

 

A Tunnel Connection can be created only when SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere tenants are “deep linked.” by either of the following 3 options:

  • Option 1 — In BDC tenant where SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere tenants are linked via BDC formation
  • Option 2 — In SAP Analytics Cloud tenant and SAP Datasphere tenant (Non BDC) are linked via UCL formation. 
  • Option 3 — SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere Tenant are linked for seamless planning  (most recommended if SAC tenant is running  on HANA Cloud)

See more detail from blog, Scheduling SAP Analytics Cloud Stories Based on SA... - SAP Community

 

Best regards,
Xiwen

entry.title: Re: Limitation - Change in Availability of Dependents Inf...
entry.content: Hello Gopi, hanks for the clarification and for sharing the documentation. I fully understand the release scope regarding CSF Dependents. However, from what I can see, this change will effectively block the use of CSF Dependents for new customers who are using Onboarding together with Employee Central. Can you please confirm if this limitation is expected to be addressed in a future release? It would be helpful to understand the roadmap here, especially for customers planning new implementations. Best regards, Sandra

entry.title: Re: MF60 can not generate replenishment with 313 movement...
entry.content:
Why the second material cannot be staged with 313 is because, movement type 313 is designed for WM‑managed storage locations, where stock is transferred from WM to a non‑WM location (interim storage type to production supply area). For a material in a non‑WM managed storage location, there is no WM process behind it, so the system raises error M7 096 because 313 is not valid there. In other words, 313 only works when the issuing storage location is WM‑managed.

The standard approach is:

Use 313 for the first step which is transfer posting from WM storage location to production supply (non‑WM location). This creates the outbound delivery and triggers WM picking. Then use 261 for the second step which is goods issue from the production supply location to the production order. For materials in non‑WM storage locations, you cannot use 313. They must be staged directly with 261 (single‑step GI). If you want all materials to follow a 2‑step process, they must be assigned to WM‑managed storage locations.

entry.title: Re: Limit warehouse order for PI document
entry.content: Jon, thank you very much, your solution helped me just in time for the 1st quarterly inventory 🙂 Highy appreciate it.

entry.title: While executing WB2R_SV for a Sales commission contract it is giving pricing error in SAP S4 hana

Hi All

In the Sandbox SAP S4 hana 2023 on premise system while executing the WB2r_SV for external Sales commission contract I am getting the below error.

Sales commission contract:

Settlement procedure

and the below is SD pricing procedure for SO and Billing

Let me know if you need any further details.

Other Information: Created the sales order, delivery and invoice and External partner also is part of the sales order and invoice.

Successfully tested the Sales Rebate testing without any error with above SD pricing procedure and relevant Rebate Settlement pricing procedure. I don't think any issues with SD pricing procedure but not sure about the Sales commission settlement procedure.

Thanks

Sri

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud enables user-based authoriza...
entry.content:
Dear expert,

In our scenario, two sales users belong to the same sales organization and are assigned the same business role.
Sales staff A is responsible for Material 1, while Sales staff B is responsible for Material 2.
Sales A shall only be authorized to edit/view the sales price of Material 1 and shall not have access to the price of Material 2.
Based on the above requirement, could you please provide more detailed information and configuration steps regarding:
2) Authorization Objects
3) Analytical Privileges
 
Best regards,
Joey

entry.title: Re: SAC Live Connection to S/4HANA Private Cloud via CORS
entry.content:
Hi,

 

Firstly, to eliminate the S/4HANA credential popup, you need to create one Live Direct connection to your S/4HANA system using Authentication Method as SAML Single Sign On, which requires that you are using same Identity Provider (IdP) for SAP Analytics Cloud and S/4HANA. 

See https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ANALYTICS_CLOUD/00f68c2e08b941f081002fd3691d86a7/c1cbf644b0a1434fbb4ea072a0562286.html

 

Meanwhile, to ensure that you could open the story from mobile using SSO, , you need to enable Advanced Feature by following steps in Live Data Connections Advanced Features Using the Cloud Connector | SAP Help Portal

 

Best regards,
Xiwen

entry.title: Missing Simulation in Workflow Configuration: Why PRs Deserve the Same Transparency as POs
entry.content:
With SAP S/4HANA release 2602, SAP introduced a highly welcome enhancement: the ability to simulate workflows for Purchase Orders (POs) in the app Manage Workflows for Purchase Orders. This new functionality provides much‑needed transparency by allowing users to see which workflow will be triggered for a given document and why.

 

However, when looking at the overall procurement process, one question naturally arises:

Why does this simulation capability stop at Purchase Orders?

Purchase Requisitions (PRs) are the starting point of the procurement lifecycle, yet the app Manage Workflows for Purchase Requisitions (F2705A) still lacks any comparable workflow simulation functionality. This blog post outlines why this distinction is problematic and why extending workflow simulation to Purchase Requisitions would be a meaningful and consistent improvement.

The Current Challenge: Limited Transparency in PR Workflow Determination

Workflow configuration for Purchase Requisitions is often complex:

  • Multiple workflow definitions may exist
  • Start conditions overlap
  • Different approval paths apply depending on value, material group, account assignment, or requester

Today, when a Purchase Requisition is created, the system determines the workflow in the background. If an unexpected workflow is triggered—or none at all—users and administrators are left with limited options:

  • Trial-and-error testing
  • Manual analysis of start conditions
  • Debugging or log interpretation

In contrast, the new workflow simulation for Purchase Orders allows users to proactively validate workflow behavior before real documents are processed.

This creates an inconsistency in tooling between two tightly related business objects.

Why Workflow Simulation Matters—Especially for Purchase Requisitions

Purchase Requisitions are not just another document type. They are:

  • The entry point of procurement
  • Often created by non-procurement users
  • The basis for downstream Purchase Orders and contracts

Introducing workflow simulation for PRs would address several real-world challenges:

1. Validation of Workflow Setup

Simulation would make it immediately clear:

  • Which workflow is selected
  • Which start conditions are fulfilled
  • Why one workflow is chosen over another

This is particularly valuable in systems where multiple workflows coexist.

2. Prevention of Incorrect Approvals

Without simulation, misconfigured workflows may only be discovered after release:

  • Wrong approvers
  • Missing approvals
  • Delays due to rework

Simulation enables preventive validation instead of reactive correction.

3. Reduced Dependency on Technical Analysis

Today, resolving workflow issues often requires:

  • Technical expertise
  • Backend analysis
  • IT involvement

A simulation feature empowers business users—especially purchasers—to validate workflows on their own.

Proposed Improvement

Extend the workflow simulation functionality introduced in release 2602 for Purchase Orders also to:

  • App: Manage Workflows for Purchase Requisitions
  • Fiori App ID: F2705A
  • Business Role: BR_PURCHASER

The simulation should:

  • Accept PR-relevant input parameters
  • Display the selected workflow
  • Show which start conditions are met
  • Clearly indicate why other workflows are not selected

In short: the same transparency that is now available for Purchase Orders should also be available for Purchase Requisitions.

Business Value and Benefits

For purchasing organizations, this enhancement would deliver tangible value:

✔️  Faster and safer workflow configuration

✔️  Higher process reliability

✔️  Fewer approval errors

✔️  Less troubleshooting effort

✔️  Better user confidence in workflow logic

Most importantly, it would ensure process consistency across the procurement lifecycle.

Call to Action: Help Improve Workflow Configuration

The introduction of workflow simulation for Purchase Orders in SAP S/4HANA 2602 is a strong step in the right direction. But by stopping at Purchase Orders, SAP leaves a critical gap at the very start of procurement.

If transparency and predictability are important for workflow configuration—and they are—then Purchase Requisitions deserve the same level of simulation support.

And if you have read this far, I encourage you to:

👉  Please Vote to Idea # 365219 Workflow Simulation for Purchase Requisitions on SAP Influence and help move this enhancement forward.

References

  • Workflow Simulation for Purchase Orders | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Re: Sizing the SAP Datasphere Object Store
entry.content:
Back in December / January we tested the object store and we couldn't make it perform when reading from it. We tried partitioning but it had no impact what so ever. We had a table with 500 mio rows and more than 400 columns and reading only 10 out of 400 did not work much faster as expected (parquet files are column organised). We gave up on the object store as we did not have the time to dig deeper. It would help us a lot if you (SAP) could give some expected performance measures, large tables, small tables, few columns, many columns, etc depending on number of CPUs. When we know what to expect we can also give feedback / create tickets on low performance, at the moment it is just a black box.

With kind regards

Kristian Appel

entry.title: Re: RISE with SAP: Multi-layer Defense in Depth Architecture of SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition

entry.title: Freight Unit Creation Using Packaging Material in SAP S/Hana Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

Good day!

I just have a question regarding freight unit creation in SAP S/4Hana Cloud Public Edition, Transportation Management.

So the scenario is that I wanted to plan transportation in cubic meter (m3) but I wanted the volume to capture the packaging material volume in Pack process instead of the total volume of the items.

Can anyone help me please? Per testing, the freight unit is only capturing the loading volume instead of the tare volume.

Thank you in advance.

VG

entry.title: Intercompany Activity Cost Allocation - is it available for S4/HANA Private Cloud
entry.content:
Hello,
I am not sure is this the correct board for this question.
We are implementing S4/HANA PCE in my company (go-live date: 01/05/2027) and there is significant interest for the IC functionality above. See also screenshot below.

I have heard the feature is currently available only for S4/HANA Public Cloud. Is this true or can this be implemented also for Private Cloud edition? 

Thank you
Rosario

entry.title: Re: [S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition] Custom Currency Code f...
entry.content:
In Public Cloud:

1. **Can we create custom currency codes (like "USDA") in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?**
- Is this supported via standard configuration or key user extensibility?

Ans: No, you cannot create custom currency codes, neither standard nor extensibility is possible

**If custom currency codes are supported, can we maintain separate exchange rate tables for them?**

**Are there any functional limitations or restrictions when using custom currency codes?**
- Are there any standard apps, processes, or integration scenarios that do not support custom currency codes?

No, as explained in Question 1, custom currency codes are not supported.

*Is there an alternative standard approach recommended by SAP for this scenario?**
- For example, using different currency types or other configuration options?

In general companies use different exchange rate for sales tax/VAT purpose for incoming and outgoing invoices. For Vendor and Customer invoice you can use BADi to have separate exchange rate at the time of invoice generation. For all other transactions, only M rate for the exchange pair will be considered.

BADI for billing documents

Badi name : Custom Data Transfer for Billing Process Documents

ID:SD_BIL_DATA_TRANSFER

Business Contexts: SD_BILLINGDOC and SD_BILLINGDOCITEM

BADI for invoicing


Badi name: Enrich Invoicing/Posting Documents of Invoicing Unit

ID:FKK_EVENT_2645

Business Contexts:CINV_INVOICING

 

Regards

Satish

entry.title: Re: Getting 403 forbidden error when fetching data using...
entry.content:
Hi,

 

It is suggested to collect HAR file as per SAP KBA 2280022 in the two scenarios 

- 3 dimensions

- 4 dimensions

to compare the response of GetResponse call sending to BW.

 

If you can observe the same error in the response of GetResponse call for 4 dimensions in the HAR file, it means that the error comes from BW system and you can copy the payload to execute it via RSBITT as per SAP KBA 3448300.

Best regards,
Xiwen

entry.title: Re: #CoE#QDGG Business function only available on 3SL.
entry.content:
Hi Jimmy,

Certain business configurations and customizations that require developer extensibility, complex localization, or advanced change management can only be fully implemented in 3SL due to its architectural advantages.

Key Distinction: The limitation is not in the business function itself, but in the implementation approach and customization depth available. 3SL enables more sophisticated adaptations of standard business processes through developer extensibility and structured testing environments.

Following please find some typical features on 3SL:

Custom Sales Order Types (Complex Logic),

Custom Pricing Procedures with Complex Calculations,

Country-Specific Tax Configurations (Non-Standard Countries),
Custom Approval Workflows with External System Integration,

Custom Production Planning Logic,

Custom Inventory Valuation Methods

 

Best regards,

Yu Yong

entry.title: Re: Agent determination by badi for non po invoices
entry.content:
Hello @MP_77,

in order to Set up Agent Determination by BADI for Supplier Invoices, you need to set up BADI  MRM_WORKFLOW_AGENTS.

Step Overview:

  1. Open App Custom Logic
  2. Create a new implementation for Extension Point ID MRM_WORKFLOW_AGENTS
  3. Check the documentation provided
  4. Then create your custom logic as needed

Please find some more info here:

  • Workflow Features for Supplier Invoices (Parked as Complete) | SAP Help Portal
  • How to Configure the Flexible Workflow for Supplier Invoices | SAP Help Portal

I hope this helps.

Best regards,

Kerisha

entry.title: Re: While executing WB2R_SV for a Sales commission contra...
entry.content: Check the Root Causes explained in SAP note 2536545

entry.title: Re: #CoE#QDGG Business function only available on 3SL.
entry.content: Thanks Yong.

entry.title: Re: best-priced items in Compare Supplier Quotation app
entry.content:
The Best-Priced Items app does not just look at the lowest price in isolation. According to SAP’s logic, the determination also considers whether the quotation item can fully cover the RFQ requirement (quantity and delivery date). If a supplier offers a lower unit price but less than the requested quantity, that item will not be flagged as “best‑priced,” because it cannot fully satisfy the requirement. In your example, Supplier A quoted only 9 EA for R001 when the RFQ asked for 10 EA. Even though the price is lower, it is not considered “best‑priced” because it doesn’t meet the full requested quantity. Supplier B, on the other hand, quoted the full quantities for all three items, so all of them are marked as best‑priced, even if the price isn’t the absolute lowest in every case.

So yes, the determination logic does factor in full delivery capability at item level, not just the price. That is why you see Supplier A flagged with fewer best‑priced items than expected.

entry.title: Re: Generating an Authorization Token in Romania’s ANAF Portal Using Postman

Thank you
Frank

entry.title: abap service instance unavailable
entry.content: PLease help me , I am getting is error on opening up the dashboard of my abap service instance , its a standard version and i have alot of applications connected to it and all have gone down .

entry.title: Re: BADI for Billing document Item Custom Field
entry.content: Use BAdI SD_BIL_DATA_TRANSFER. This is the cloud‑released BAdI for billing document creation. It lets you map or overwrite fields when data is transferred from the reference document (sales order, delivery) to the billing document. You can extend the billing item CDS view with your custom fields and then populate them in the BAdI implementation. For more information, check this SAP document  Custom Data Transfer for Billing Process Documents

entry.title: Re: [S4HANA Cloud] How to automatically suggest default s...
entry.content: Thank you for your reply. So, with the standard functionality, the production storage location in the MRP area profile of a child item isn't referenced, and only the item master and BOM details of the child item are referenced, is that correct? So, the only options are to manage multiple versions of the BOM or implement custom logic?

entry.title: Cost center master data maintenance Fiori
entry.content:
Cost center master data maintenance Fiori

Is there an SAP Standard solution on Maintenance of Cost center master data (single/multiple) in Fiori with workflow approval without using MDG as we're not using MDG.

New cost centers should also be added in an alternative cost center hierarchy.

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud enables user-based authoriza...
entry.content:
Hello @Joey3,

in order to achieve this, you can work with Authorization Groups.

  1. These Groups need to be assigned to be set up.
  2. After the creation of Authorization Groups, they can be asisgned to Material Masters (=Products) /  Product Groups / Product Types.
  3. As a last step, you can maintain these Authorization Groups as Restrictions in app Maintain Business Roles to define which role will be able to access which elements.

 

‼️ However, in your case you mentioned Sales staff A and Sales staff B are using the same business role.

To work with Authorization Groups, you would need to set up two different business roles:

  • Business role A with access to Material 1
  • Business Role B with access to Material 2

 

Here are some links regarding the topic:

  • 2808362 - Restriction "Authorization Group for Product Group" ignored when using the App "Manage Product Master Data" - SAP for Me
  • 3203535 - Not possible to maintain values for restriction types 'Authorization Group for Product' and 'Authorization Group for Product Type' - SAP for Me

 

I hope this helps.

Best regards,

Kerisha

entry.title: Re: best-priced items in Compare Supplier Quotation app
entry.content: If both Supplier A and Supplier B fail to meet the full RFQ quantity, then neither quotation item is considered “best‑priced” in the strict sense. The app does not select the one that is closer to the requested quantity;  it requires the full quantity to be offered. In that case, neither A (8EA) nor B (9EA) would be flagged as best‑priced for that item

entry.title: Harnessing SAP Business Data Cloud for the administrator persona
entry.content:
Working with SAP Partners on SAP Business Data Cloud for the administrator role brings a very concrete set of tasks that often get overlooked until go-live.

In this session, we walk through how administration looks in real projects, from provisioning to connectivity with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition.

We will cover what's new in the landscape, how SAP for Me supports provisioning, and how integration between S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and SAP Business Data Cloud is set up.  Plus a live Q&A to address practical questions.

The thing is, many discussions stay at concept level.  Here we focus on what you do: moving from customer landscape view to instantiation, setting trust, and ensuring bi-directional connectivity works as expected.

Finally, you will be able to guide customer conversations on administration topics and explain the current landscape changes with confidence.

Register now  https://partneredge.sap.com/en/library/education/psd/2025/sep/e_oe_te_w_PSD_WEB_00011315.html

#SAP #SAPBTP #SAPPartners #SAPEcosystem #SAPBusinessDataCloud #SAPCloud #DataManagement

entry.title: Re: My Inbox Build Process Automation Stuck Loading
entry.content: yes we are also facing the same issue , so what is the latest work around solution ?

entry.title: Re: bapi_po_create1 error
entry.content: thankyou it worked

entry.title: Re: PO creator should have access to return PR
entry.content:
In SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, the “return PR” functionality you are describing (similar to Oracle, where a PO creator can send a requisition back) is not available. The flexible workflow framework covers approval, rejection, and resubmission, but it does not provide a direct “return to PR” action from the PO side.

Some workarounds are

  • You can configure rejection steps in the PO workflow so that the requisition creator is notified and can adjust the PR. This is the closest equivalent to “return PR.”
  • If you need a true “return PR” button, you can try to implement an extension (via in‑app extensibility or side‑by‑side BTP extension) that triggers a workflow event to reopen the PR.

entry.title: TRM | Update Type SE1000 "Fixed" but no FI Document (Z-Copy Works) | S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

 

I am facing a strange issue in SAP Treasury and Risk Management (S/4HANA) where the standard Update Type SE1000 (Purchase) is successfully "Fixed" in the subledger, but no accounting entry is generated in FI.

 

Scenario Details:

 

Product Type: 02B (Investment in MF Debt)

 

Transaction Type: 100 (Purchase)

 

Company Code: 5200

 

Valuation Area: 001 (Operative Valuation Area - IAS)

 

The Problem:

 

When running TBB1 for a Purchase transaction (Update Type SE1000):

 

The Test Run is green and says "Test run was successful."

 

The Update Run marks the flow as "Fixed" in TPM13.

 

No FI Document is generated.

 

TPM20 (Posting Journal) is completely blank for this transaction.

 

SLG1 shows no error logs in the database.

 

SM13 shows no update terminations.

 

What I have verified:

 

Account Determination: Assigned and correct. Account symbols have valid G/L mappings.

 

Posting Relevance: SE1000 is marked as "Relevant for Posting" in SPRO.

 

FI Side: Periods are open (OB52) and Number Ranges are maintained (FBN1).

 

AAR: Account Assignment Reference is correctly maintained on the Administration tab.

 

The "Z" Breakthrough:

 

I created a custom Update Type ZSE1000 as a copy of SE1000 with the exact same settings. When using ZSE1000, the system successfully generates the posting log and FI document.

 

Questions:

 

Why does the standard SE1000 skip the accounting interface and go directly to "Fixed" status while a Z-copy works?

 

Is there a technical classification for standard update types that might suppress FI posting for SE1000?

 

Any insights or suggestions on where to look further would be greatly appreciated.

 

Note- For same product and setting, sale (200) working fine but not purchase  (100)

entry.title: Re: Wrong due date calculation
entry.content: Screen shot is missing. Moreover, your post does not clearly specify whether the issue is on the purchase side or sales side. When you create a discussion, please give as much information as possible for better understanding

entry.title: CDS for Billing document Business Place
entry.content:
Hi,

I'm working on Billing document form and I need to display field 'Business Place'. The field can be see at Billing document Header -> Taxes section.  I tried look into several Billing related CDS like I_BillingDocument or I_BillingDocumentBasic. But Field BusinessPlace is not available.  In old core SAP table the field would store as VBRK-BUPLA. But it seems missing in the Billing CDS. Billing Documen

Thank you very much in advance.

entry.title: Re: HANA does not retain table partitioning when a column...
entry.content:
Please refer the details from KBA 2044468  - FAQ: SAP HANA Partitioning ->

19. Is the partitioning information kept during transports?

Partitioning information is not available in ABAP DDIC. Therefore transports don't consider it and partitioning is not transported. This means that you have to activate partitioning in all involved systems of your ABAP system landscape individually.

Hope this is helpful.

~Gaurav

entry.title: SAP CX Product Inspiration Series 2026: Execute CX as your Growth Stratetegy
entry.content:
Customer experience has entered a new era. In 2026, engagement alone is no longer enough. Businesses win by executing with intelligence, speed, and context across the entire enterprise. With AI embedded directly into sales, service, commerce, and engagement workflows, SAP Customer Experience is transforming CX from a front-office function into a true growth engine of your company. In this new Product Inspiration Series, we explore how SAP CX brings executional AI into the flow of work by connecting customer signals to real outcomes, powered by the business data and processes that run your organization.
The special character of the SAP CX Product Inspiration Series remains unchanged. Each video:
  • spotlights concrete innovations across the SAP CX portfolio
  • is created by product and industry experts, for fellow experts
  • opens with a short Q&A to set the context—the why, what, and how
  • follows a zero‑slides approach, focusing entirely on the product itself
  • delivers in‑depth explorations and hands‑on demos
  • helps you better understand the Consumer Industries and SAP CX product strategy
  • is crafted with passion and dedication for you—our customers, partners, colleagues, and CX enthusiasts

Now, let’s take a closer look at the first Product Inspiration videos that have crossed the finish line…

1. Gainsight Customer Success Management for SAP Sales Cloud Version 2

 

Join Emma Falldorf, Product Manager SAP Sales Cloud Version 2 at SAP, for an exciting overview of the latest integrations between Gainsight Customer Success Management and SAP Sales Cloud Version 2. In this insightful video, we dive deep into the key benefits and usage scenarios of this powerful integration, provide a comprehensive look at how Gainsight enhances the customer experience.

The key highlights shared in this video are:
00:00 - Introduction
00:39 - Why did SAP invest in bringing Gainsight into SAP Sales Cloud Version 2?
02:06 - Which concrete scenarios does Gainsight offer for SAP customers?
03:09 - How can customers adopt Gainsight in SAP Sales Cloud Version 2?
04:16 - Start of the Demo
04:32 - Demo: Sales-to-Success hand-over in SAP Sales Cloud Version 2
06:54 - Demo: Customer Success Management in Gainsight
10:27 - Summary: Intelligent CX from SAP

 

2. Account Planning in SAP Sales Cloud Version 2 in Action

Join Crystal Zhang, Product Manager SAP Sales Cloud Version 2 at SAP, for an exciting overview of the latest Account Planning capabilities of SAP Sales Cloud Version 2.
In this SAP Product Inspiration Video, learn why intelligent account planning is essential for modern sales teams and how SAP Sales Cloud Version 2 brings structure, insight, and focus to key account management. Through a concise end-to-end demo, the video shows how sales teams can capture customer goals and insights, define strategies and sales plans, monitor pipeline health, collaborate through action plans, and generate AI-powered executive summaries—all within one integrated solution. Discover how Intelligent CX from SAP helps turn account insights into consistent, measurable sales success.

The key highlights shared in this video are:
00:00 - Introduction
00:56 - Why Account Planning in SAP Sales Cloud Version 2?
02:19 - What scenarios does Account Planning support?
03:37 - How can customers adopt Account Planning?
04:26 - Start of the Demo
04:42 - Demo: Account Plan - Set Customer Goals, Utilize Notes and Surveys
05:56 - Demo: Account Plan - Define Business Figures
06:18 - Demo: Account Plan - Define Key Stakeholders
06:49 - Demo: Account Plan - Conduct SWAT Analysis
07:07 - Demo: Account Plan - Define Sales Plans and Targets
08:00 - Demo: Account Plan - Monitor Pipeline Coverage
08:13 - Demo: Account Plan - Specify Action Plans
08:43 - Demo: Account Plan - Track Progress in a Gantt Chart
09:00 - Demo: Account Plan - Generate AI-based Executive Summary
09:43 - Summary: Intelligent CX from SAP

 

3. AI Scouting in SAP Sports One

Join Michael Kesselmann, Product Manager SAP Sports One, and Achim Ittner, VP for Sports at SAP, for an exciting overview on embedded AI scouting capabilities in SAP Sports One. In this video, you will learn how clubs and federations can leverage Generative AI to streamline scouting workflows, accelerate decision-making, and create high‑quality player summaries with ease.
Discover how SAP Joule enhances the scouting process by consolidating extensive written reports, answering performance-related questions instantly, and generating structured management summaries that support faster and more informed squad planning decisions.

The key highlights shared in this video are:
00:00 - Introduction
02:54 - Start of the Demo
03:38 - Demo: Scouting Reports in SAP Sports One
04:09 - Demo: AI-Assisted Scouting with Joule in SAP Sports One
05:05 - Summary: Intelligent CX from SAP

 

If you have any further questions please let me know. You can find more videos here as well as on our YouTube channel.

Bookmark this site as further videos will be published soon. 🚀

entry.title: Re: BW Modernisation with the SAP BW Data Product Generator
entry.content: What is the exact advantage of using this in comparison to taking the view as a remote table direktly?
